slots.rod: Rivertex Zeus 8'10" SE (Spinning Rod, X-Heavy, 3/4-2 1/8 Oz, 18-46 Lb, Mod Fast)

leader_recommendation.why: Essential for Northern Pike and Muskie bite-off protection. Casting Spoon 21g #3/0 is ubersheet-recommended for Smallmouth Bass and Yellow Perch.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Cast along weed edges and rocky points. Vary retrieve speed  -  slow for Walleye, medium for bass, fast for pike.

issues: Casting Spoon 3/4 Oz #2/0 is close to ubersheet-recommended Casting Spoon 21g #3/0 for Smallmouth Bass  -  effective
41.8 Lb mono handles all spinning targets comfortably (Northern Pike max 48.5 lb at ~87% tension)
Zeus is X-Heavy power  -  ideal for the larger predators at Saint-Croix
improvements: Excellent active predator rod  -  cast along structure for Smallmouth Bass ($73/lb) and Walleye ($86/lb)
Northern Pike ($77/lb, max 48.5 lb) will hit this spoon aggressively  -  best income per cast
Tiger Trout ($70/lb) also responds to Bullet Spinner 24g #2/0 per ubersheet  -  consider swapping if targeting trout
Troll from the Draco along deep weed edges for Northern Pike and Smallmouth Bass
Dawn/dusk casting near rocky points is prime Walleye time

slots.rod: Heavy Chaser 9'10" SE (Bottom Rod, X-Heavy, Casting 6-8 4/5 Oz, 22-56.2 Lb)
slots.terminal_tackle: Open Feeder 4 1/2 Oz, Simple Bell, Hook #10/0
slots.bait: Medium Cutbait (confirmed 2026-04-08 - consistent Blue Catfish catches)

leader_recommendation.why: Titanium protects against catfish whisker abrasion and occasional pike strikes on bottom bait. The .014" test is appropriate for the 50 Lb main line.

issues: CONFIRMED (4/8): Medium Cutbait + #6/0 hook = consistent Blue Catfish catches. Bait swap validated!
Hook downsized from #10/0 to #6/0  -  improved hookup rate confirmed in 4/8 afternoon session
The feeder with groundbait outperforms sinker-only rods  -  scent attraction draws fish in
50 Lb line handles Blue Catfish (91.5 lb max) at high tension  -  manageable with patience and loose drag
10 catfish caught in afternoon session including Trophy Blue Catfish 42.5 lb
improvements: Medium Cutbait + #6/0 hook is the CONFIRMED catfish combo  -  do not change
Medium Cutbait also catches Lake Sturgeon  -  dual-purpose bait
Keep feeder setup  -  outperforms sinker-only rods consistently
Deploy on RodPod Trio at Fairytale Docks  -  feeder scent draws fish to the entire rod pod area
NIGHT SESSIONS: Blue Catfish become very active  -  this rod should be soaking all night
Alternate with active Triple Runner lure fishing for 10x XP bonus on lure catches

leader_recommendation.material: Fluorocarbon .033" (currently equipped)  -  consider upgrading to Titanium
leader_recommendation.why: Fluoro leader is invisible underwater which helps with wary fish, but Northern Pike and Muskie have razor teeth  -  a titanium leader would prevent bite-offs on these predators.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 8-15 ft for muskie/pike cruising mid-depth. At Fairytale Docks, try 10-12 ft near the boat structures.

issues: Fluorocarbon leader instead of titanium  -  risk of bite-off from Northern Pike, Muskie, Clear Muskie, and Tiger Muskie
Shiners covers Blue Catfish, all 3 Muskie species, Northern Pike, and Smallmouth Bass  -  excellent bait choice
79 Lb line on L'Impulsion handles everything including Blue Catfish (91.5 lb) at ~86% tension
Hook #8/0 is appropriate for large predators
improvements: MONSTER FLOAT RIG  -  your heaviest passive setup, proven at this location
STRONGLY recommend swapping fluoro leader to titanium  -  pike/muskie will bite through fluoro
Shiners is the ideal bait here  -  covers 6 high-value species simultaneously
Deploy on RodPod at Fairytale Docks  -  your journal notes the area near the boats is productive
Blue Catfish (91.5 lb) is the biggest target  -  tight fight on 79 Lb line but landable with patience
Clear Muskie ($45/lb, max 69.5 lb) at ~88% tension  -  exciting but risky without titanium leader

leader_recommendation.why: Titanium protects against pike. Small Minnows is one of the most versatile baits at Saint-Croix  -  covers 9 species!
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float to 4-8 ft near shore structure. For Atlantic Salmon try deeper (8-12 ft).

issues: Small Minnows covers 9 species  -  BEST species coverage of any single bait at Saint-Croix!
Hook #1/0 is appropriate for the mid-size species targeted
10 Lb line limits what you can land  -  Northern Pike (48.5 lb max) will break this easily
Titanium .004" (7 Lb) is the weakest link  -  pike will pull against this
Chubby float is good for the smaller to medium species
improvements: EXCELLENT SPECIES COVERAGE  -  Small Minnows targets: American Shad, Atlantic Salmon, Brown Trout, Freshwater Drum, Northern Pike, Smallmouth Bass, Tiger Trout, Walleye, White Bass
Major upgrade from the old Bloodworms setup  -  went from 3 species to 9 species!
Walleye ($86/lb) and Smallmouth Bass ($73/lb) on a float with Small Minnows is a proven technique
Atlantic Salmon ($45/lb, max 13.4 lb) is within the rod's capability  -  nice bonus catches
Deploy on RodPod for passive fishing at shore  -  let the float soak and collect species
If a pike hits, loosen drag immediately  -  you won't land a big one but might save the leader

slots.rod: Rivertex DracoSpin 8' (Spinning Rod, X-Heavy, 18-40 Lb, Fast)

leader_recommendation.why: Titanium is essential  -  pike and muskie can hit crayfish jigs. The .014" handles the heavy jig action well.

issues: Crayfish 7.5-10cm is ubersheet-recommended for Blue Catfish and Brown Bullhead  -  your 4" Crayfish should trigger similar strikes
Jighead 18g #6/0 matches the ubersheet JigHead 18g #6/0 for Blue Catfish  -  PERFECT MATCH
41 Lb fluoro is well-suited for the targets  -  Blue Catfish (91.5 lb max) will be a challenge but manageable on smaller specimens
DracoSpin's X-Heavy power handles the heavy jig presentations well
improvements: ACTIVE CATFISH/BASS HUNTER  -  Crayfish jig is the ubersheet-recommended approach for Blue Catfish
Your journal confirms: moderate success casting crayfish around the dock/boat area for bass, walleye, and trout
Hop the crayfish jig along the bottom near structure  -  Blue Catfish and Smallmouth Bass both respond
Dawn/dusk: focus on rocky points for Walleye using slow jig hops
Night: Blue Catfish become very active  -  this jig in deep water near bottom is deadly
Consider carrying a Bony Shad or Worm 3" as backup soft plastic for different species

slots.rod: Rivertex Salmonster 8' (Spinning Rod, X-Heavy, 18-40 Lb, Mod Fast)

leader_recommendation.why: Titanium essential for crankbait fishing  -  pike and muskie strike crankbaits aggressively and will bite through non-metal leaders instantly.

issues: Triple Runner 8 ft #5/0 is the CONFIRMED #1 lure at Saint-Croix  -  ~80% hit rate across multiple sessions
LURE COMPARISON (4/8): Triple Runner (~80%) vs Hunched 8' (~40-50%) vs Tweaker 16' (~40-50%)  -  Triple Runner wins decisively
Do NOT swap to other lures  -  Hunched and Tweaker tested and rejected
Catches Smallmouth Bass, Blue Catfish (including trophies!), trout, and Trophy Northern Pike (21.8 lb)
41 Lb fluoro on Kraken 8000 SE handles all crankbait targets comfortably
Salmonster + Kraken is a strong combination for sustained cranking and the drop-jig technique
improvements: YOUR #1 ACTIVE ROD  -  Triple Runner with drop-jig technique is the highest-producing setup at Saint-Croix
TECHNIQUE: Drop to bottom â†’ rest 3s â†’ reel level 3s â†’ drop/jig to bottom â†’ rest 3s â†’ smooth reel. Hits come on the draw-up.
XP BONUS: Lure catches give ~10x XP vs bait (1,382 XP vs ~100-150 XP per fish)  -  confirmed 4/8
Targets fish 3-5 ft from bottom in 20-40 ft water  -  where most fish hold at Fairytale Docks
Cast toward the central dock with two player markers (~328 ft) for best results
Outperforms 3 passive bottom rods combined (40+ active catches vs ~10 passive per session)
Alternate between passive bait rods and active Triple Runner for maximum XP/hour

slots.rod: Mokonzi 12'10" SE (Bottom Rod, X-Heavy, Casting 6-9 7/8 Oz, 39.7-81.6 Lb)
slots.bait: Medium Cutbait (confirmed 2026-04-08 - consistent Blue Catfish catches)

leader_recommendation.why: Titanium withstands catfish whiskers and bottom abrasion. Heavy sinker holds bottom in current.

issues: CONFIRMED (4/8): Medium Cutbait swap validated  -  consistent catches alongside slot 2
79 Lb line on Fileur 11000 SE handles Blue Catfish (91.5 lb) at ~86% tension  -  tough but proven
8 Oz sinker holds bottom well in deeper water  -  good for 150 ft+ casts from the dock
Keep #8/0 hook on this rod  -  good balance between hookup rate and trophy capability
Sinker-only rod still underperforms feeder rod (slot 2)  -  no scent attraction, but still catches well with correct bait
improvements: CONFIRMED (4/8): Medium Cutbait swap working  -  consistent catfish catches alongside slot 2
Medium Cutbait + #8/0 hook is the confirmed combo for this rod  -  do not change
Cast toward sturgeon marker [299.82, -161.17] during 7-8 AM and 5-9 PM feed windows
Your journal confirms: 42.8 lb sturgeon caught on Duck Mussel Meat at this marker location on 4/8
Consider alternating: Medium Cutbait for general fishing, Duck Mussel Meat during sturgeon windows
Deploy on RodPod Trio at Fairytale Docks alongside the feeder rod for maximum passive coverage
STURGEON TIMING: 7-8 AM and 5-9 PM ONLY  -  plan your dock sessions around these windows

gear.boat: Garry Scott Draco Bass Boat  -  USABLE! Boat fishing allowed at Saint-Croix
notes: Draco Bass Boat for reaching mid-lake structure  -  sonar for muskie, pike, and walleye
Luminos Headlamp for night Blue Catfish sessions
RodPod Trio  -  deploy bottom rods (slots 2 & 7) at Fairytale Docks, then fish actively with slots 1, 5, 6
FishStronghold M Plus  -  fill with Northern Pike ($77/lb) and Walleye ($86/lb) for maximum income
ChumChest Lux for groundbait deployment near bottom rigs
Lucky Seven tackle box for lure/bait variety in the field

groundbait.status: MyFeeder recipe  -  Catfish Destroyer (Bigstream Spicy Golden aroma, Citrake-Pea Golden particles)
notes: Catfish Destroyer deployed via Closed Feeder on slot 2  -  attracts Blue Catfish to the bottom rig zone
Great synergy: Feeder on slot 2 draws catfish, sinker on slot 7 holds bait in the same area
Consider switching to generic fish feeder base if targeting non-catfish species from shore

boat.license_required: Advanced Michigan License ($8,000/day, $22,800/3 days, $50,400/week, 600 BC unlimited)  -  required for boat + night fishing

recommendation.speed: Bass boat with motor and sonar
recommendation.features: 24.4 ft aluminium bass boat with advanced sonar, rod holders, and trolling motor. Essential for reaching mid-lake muskie and pike structure.
recommendation.why: Saint-Croix Lake is large and deep  -  muskie, pike, and walleye hold on mid-lake structure that's inaccessible from shore. The Draco's sonar lets you find the underwater humps, weed edges, and rocky points where trophy fish lurk.

fishing_tips: Use sonar to locate deep structure  -  muskie and pike hold near underwater humps, weed edges, and lily pad borders
PIKE ALLEY: Position Draco off lily pads at King of Green Bay. Cast NNW with slot 3 (Triple Runner) and slot 5 (Crankbait 8ft). This is the #1 muskie zone.
Troll slot 3 (Salmonster + Triple Runner) and slot 5 (Zeus + Crankbait) along weed edges for pike and muskie
Cast slot 2 (DracoSpin + Crayfish jig) toward rocky bottoms for Smallmouth Bass and Blue Catfish
Muskie are rare  -  'fish of 10,000 casts'  -  but three species increases your odds
Northern Pike ($77/lb) are more common and nearly as valuable  -  target weed edges
Set up RodPod Trio at docks with slots 1, 4, 6 (bottom rods + Medium Cutbait) for passive catfish income while boating
Return to shore every 20-30 min to check passive rods and sell keepnet
For Muskie Golem fight: return to SHORE at Pike Alley east end  -  do NOT fight from kayak/boat
boat.cost_analysis: Advanced License $8,000/day for boat + night. Travel $7,800 + $2,000/day extend. Total 3-day trip: ~$30,400. One trophy Northern Pike ($77/lb x 48.5 lb = $3,738) + one trophy Walleye ($86/lb x 23.2 lb = $1,998) pays for the trip.

passive_fishing.best_stand_spot: Fairytale Docks for catfish income, King of the Green Bay docks for muskie targeting
passive_fishing.stand_spot_why: Fairytale Docks is the proven catfish zone (50 fish/903 lb in one afternoon trip on 4/8). King of the Green Bay has Trophy Pike and Muskellunge from old wooden docks, with Pike Alley accessible by boat for premium muskie action.
passive_fishing.active_fishing_zone: At Fairytale: Triple Runner drop-jig technique. At King of Green Bay: cast along lily pad edges for muskie. From Draco: troll crankbaits along weed edges and lily pads at Pike Alley.
strategy: CATFISH MODE (Fairytale Docks): Deploy RodPod with slots 1, 4, 6 (three bottom/feeder rods + Medium Cutbait #6/0). Active fish with slot 3 (Triple Runner). Fill keeper in ~1.5 hrs.
Slot 1 (Cat Boxer + Sinker 5.5 Oz + Medium Cutbait + Hook #6/0): Cast 100-150 ft  -  Blue Catfish
Slot 4 (Mokonzi + Sinker 8 Oz + Medium Cutbait + Hook #6/0): Cast 150 ft toward deeper water  -  Blue Catfish
Slot 6 (Heavy Chaser + Feeder + Medium Cutbait + Hook #6/0): Cast 100-150 ft  -  feeder scent attracts fish to the rod pod zone
Slot 3 (Salmonster + Triple Runner 8ft #5/0): Active fishing  -  drop-jig technique (~80% hit rate, 10x XP bonus)

MUSKIE MODE (King of Green Bay): Deploy RodPod with bottom rods for catfish income. Active cast slots 3 and 5 (crankbaits) along lily pad edges. Float rod slot 7 at ~5 ft depth with Shiners/Large Minnows near structure.
PIKE ALLEY (Boat): Take Draco, position off lily pads, cast NNW with crankbaits. Best muskie concentration on the lake.
STURGEON WINDOWS: 7-8 AM and 5-9 PM  -  be at the docks with bottom rods during these times
NIGHT STRATEGY: Blue Catfish peak activity. Deploy all bottom rods at dock. Walleye also feeds at night.
income_estimate: Northern Pike ($77/lb, max 48.5 lb)  -  up to $3,738 per trophy! Best total income per fish.
Walleye ($86/lb, max 23.2 lb)  -  up to $1,998 per trophy. Highest $/lb at Saint-Croix.
Smallmouth Bass ($73/lb, max 11.9 lb)  -  up to $862 per trophy. Good active fishing target.
Tiger Trout ($70/lb, max 6.6 lb)  -  up to $463 per trophy. Nice bonus catches.
Brown Trout ($68/lb, max 15.7 lb)  -  up to $1,067 per trophy. Trolling target.
Tiger Muskie ($54/lb, max 33.1 lb)  -  up to $1,800 per trophy. Most common muskie.
Muskie/Clear Muskie ($45/lb, max 65-69.5 lb)  -  up to $3,150 per trophy! Rare but massive.
Blue Catfish ($32/lb, max 91.5 lb)  -  up to $2,903 per trophy. Night bottom rig  -  your most CONSISTENT earner.
Passive income: $500-$2,000/hr from dock rods (catfish + muskie/pike hits)
Active trolling: $2,000-$5,000/hr targeting pike + walleye + salmon with spoon and crankbait from the Draco
Combined: $2,500-$7,000 per in-game hour. HIGHEST INCOME POTENTIAL OF ANY LOCATION.

loadout_progress.improvement_summary: Significant loadout improvement! Replaced the weak OmniFloat/Flies setup (4/10) with Salmonster/Crankbait (9/10). Added Mokonzi as a second heavy bottom rod. Fenix upgraded from Bloodworms (3 species) to Small Minnows (9 species). Added DracoSpin with ubersheet-matched Crayfish jig for active catfish hunting. Now have 3 active spinning rods for boat trolling instead of 2.

fish_coverage.Lake Sturgeon: partial  -  Duck Mussel Meat confirmed (42.8 lb caught 4/8). Medium Cutbait also on bait list.

missing_setups: Rock Bass ($54/lb) needs Night Crawlers or Leeches  -  or Nano Spinner/Craw jig on a light rod
Pumpkinseed needs Dough Balls, Semolina Balls, Red Worms, or Maggots  -  brief bait swap on slot 4
Green Sunfish needs Semolina Balls, Marshmallows, Wax Worms, or Bloodworms  -  brief bait swap on slot 4
Golden Shiner needs Dough Balls or Semolina Balls  -  brief bait swap
Bluntnose Minnow needs Bloodworms or Red Worms  -  brief bait swap on slot 4
NOTE: These are all low-value panfish  -  prioritize catching them once for first-catch XP bonus, then return to high-value targets
xp_optimization: 22 species available  -  catch ALL for massive first-catch XP bonuses
Current loadout covers 17/22 species  -  up from 16/22! Small Minnows on slot 4 added White Bass, Freshwater Drum, American Shad
Three types of Muskie at one location is RARE  -  catch all three for bragging rights + huge XP
Fish all 6 spots for location discovery XP
Night fishing unlocks Blue Catfish peak activity  -  massive XP from trophies
For remaining 5 species: bring Semolina Balls and Bloodworms/Red Worms for a panfish completion session
income_optimization: Walleye at $86/lb is the highest $/lb  -  target with Crankbait (slot 6) and Casting Spoon (slot 1) at dawn/dusk
Northern Pike at $77/lb (max 48.5 lb = $3,738 per trophy!)  -  troll with Casting Spoon and Crankbait from the Draco
Smallmouth Bass at $73/lb  -  solid income while trolling spoons
Tiger Trout at $70/lb and Brown Trout at $68/lb  -  crankbait trolling bonus catches
Blue Catfish at $32/lb but massive size (91.5 lb max = $2,903)  -  your most consistent earner from dock bottom rigs
Fill keepnet with Walleye + Northern Pike for highest average $/lb
Saint-Croix is expensive ($7,800 travel + $2,000/day + $8,000/day advanced license) but income potential is the highest of any location
priority_changes: DONE (4/8): Medium Cutbait swap CONFIRMED  -  consistent Blue Catfish catches on both bottom rods. Trophy 42.5 lb caught!
DONE (4/8): Hook #6/0 on slot 2  -  improved hookup rate confirmed
DONE (4/8): Lure tested  -  Triple Runner 8ft #5/0 (~80%) beats Hunched 8' and Tweaker 16' (~40-50%). Stick with Triple Runner.
CRITICAL: Swap slot 3 leader from Fluorocarbon to Titanium  -  pike and muskie WILL bite through fluoro
Keep Duck Mussel Meat on Cat Boxer  -  confirmed sturgeon catcher at marker [299.82, -161.17]
XP STRATEGY: Alternate passive bait rods + active Triple Runner  -  lure catches give ~10x XP (1,382 vs ~100-150 per fish)
Glow soft baits replace standard variants  -  same effectiveness, saves inventory space
For species completion: bring Semolina Balls (Pumpkinseed + Golden Shiner + Green Sunfish) and Bloodworms/Red Worms (Bluntnose Minnow + Yellow Perch)
Rock Bass ($54/lb) is the highest-value missing species  -  Night Crawlers or Leeches on a float rod would cover it
fight_techniques: UNIVERSAL TECHNIQUE - Slow Drop + Fast Raise: Drop lure to bottom, pause 4 sec, raise to level and swim 4 sec, repeat. Aggressive bottom fish (bass, walleye) ambush on the popup from rest. Works on all spinning and casting rods.

HEAVY FISH ON LIGHT ROD TECHNIQUE (GalaxyCast 8'6" vs 20+ lb Blue Catfish): Set line tension to 90% (one notch before break limit). Reel at Med-Fast speed (NOT full speed - full speed triggers max fish resistance). Pull back on rod then reel during the pull-forward. Retrieval rate ~1 ft per 2 sec (100-120 ft cast = 1-2 min fight). The GalaxyCast 8'6" is the longest X-Heavy casting rod in the game - no upgrade exists.

REEL SPEED vs FISH AGGRESSION: Fish fight intensity scales with reel speed. Dialing reel speed from Fast to Med-Fast dramatically reduces strain on rod/reel/line. Use this on any rod when fighting fish near the rod power limit.

SPOT OPTIMIZATION - Floating Dock > Fairytale Docks: Bottom rods at 75-125 ft (vs 150-250 at Fairytale) = faster retrieval, no dual-hookup timeouts. 4-Rod Stand on mid-dock facing shore covers the entire catfish/sturgeon strip. 360-degree lure fishing possible.

muskie_strategy.overview: Saint-Croix has 3 muskie species plus the Muskie Golem (monster fish). Muskie are ambush predators - expect long waits (1.5+ in-game hours between bites). Keep bottom rods soaking for catfish income between muskie hits.

Muskie.best_lure: Golem Lure 2m #8/0 (mission reward) or Triple Runner 8ft #5/0

Clear Muskie.notes: Found near Tiger Muskie zones - try areas adjacent to Sandy Cape and Pike Alley

Tiger Muskie.notes: Sandy Cape 'little rock' spot and Pike Alley corner - cast 30-38m toward right-hand reeds

king_of_the_green_bay.description: South-east bank, Greenpoint Bay. Remote spot with old wooden docks. Trophy Northern Pike and Muskellunge territory.
king_of_the_green_bay.pike_alley: Best muskie zone - at the far end of Green Bay along lily pad edges. Best accessed by boat/kayak, position just off the lily pads. Cast north-northwest toward fish activity.
king_of_the_green_bay.shore_vs_boat: Shore fishing works from the docks - good for passive bottom rods + active casting. For Pike Alley specifically, use the Draco to position off lily pads. For the Muskie Golem fight, fish from SHORE (not kayak) - the Golem runs along shore to snag your line.
king_of_the_green_bay.technique_lure: 4/0 jighead + 10cm grass green shad, Stop-and-Go or Twitching at speed 2-3. Triple Runner also works - cast along lily pad edges. Poppers effective for muskie too.
king_of_the_green_bay.technique_float: #6/0 or #7/0 hook, Shiners or Large Minnows, float depth ~5 ft. Cast toward shore features (houses, docks, lily pads).
strategy: Deploy RodPod at docks with bottom rods (slots 1, 4, 6) soaking Medium Cutbait for catfish income
Active fish with slot 3 (Salmonster + Triple Runner) and slot 5 (Zeus + Crankbait) along lily pad edges
Float rod slot 7 (EquatorMatch + Shiners/Large Minnows) set at ~5 ft depth near structure
TITANIUM LEADER REQUIRED on all rods targeting muskie - they WILL bite through fluorocarbon
Morning windows (6-9 AM) and evening windows (5-8 PM) are prime muskie time
Between muskie windows, focus on catfish income from bottom rods
When ready for Pike Alley: take Draco, position off lily pads, cast NNW with crankbaits and jigs

golem_lure.name: Golem Lure 2m #8/0 (Pike color)
how_to_get: Open Missions menu - find Muskie Golem / Fish Monsters mission chain
Track the monster mission, then travel to Saint-Croix
A lure sub-mission appears: catch 5 Backlash fish (all from Saint-Croix)
Ensure 5+ free inventory slots before completing
warnings: ALWAYS use Titanium leader with Golem Lure - bite-off = lure lost
If lost BEFORE catching Golem: redo backlash mission for another lure
If lost AFTER catching Golem: GONE PERMANENTLY - no recovery

golem_fight.location: Pike Alley (east end, shore fishing recommended)
golem_fight.fight_duration: 20-30 in-game minutes with runs to 400 ft
tips: Fish from SHORE, not kayak - Golem charges at you and runs along shore to snag line
Manage rod angle carefully for constant line tension
Golem is 'smart' - charges straight at you to create slack, uses twists/turns
Use your strongest reel (L'Impulsion 12000 SE at 81.5 Lb drag or Fileur 11000 SE at 79.4 Lb)
Kraken 8000 SE on Salmonster should handle it but will be a tough fight

steps: Track Fish Monsters / Muskie Golem mission in Missions menu
Complete lure sub-mission: catch 5 Backlash fish at Saint-Croix
Fish Pike Alley from shore with Golem Lure on titanium leader
Land the Muskie Golem (~77 lb, 20-30 min fight)
